content: Treatment of deficiency (25-OH D <20 ng/mL): adults - cholecalciferol 50,000 IU weekly × 8 weeks or 6000 IU daily, then maintenance 1500-2000 IU daily; alternative ergocalciferol 50,000 IU weekly × 8 weeks. Obese patients, malabsorption syndromes, or medications affecting vitamin D metabolism (anticonvulsants, glucocorticoids, rifampin) may require 2-3× higher doses. Monitor 25-OH D after 3-4 months, adjust to maintain level ≥30 ng/mL (some guidelines accept ≥20 ng/mL). Maintenance for sufficiency: 600-800 IU/day (IOM) or 1500-2000 IU/day (Endocrine Society) for adults; higher for elderly, dark-skinned individuals, limited sun exposure. Toxicity rare, usually from excessive supplementation (>10,000 IU/day long-term): hypercalcemia, hypercalciuria, nephrocalcinosis. Upper tolerable limit 4000 IU/day for adults without monitoring. Calcitriol reserved for hypoparathyroidism, CKD stage 4-5.
